name: Mu Shu Armadillo
recipeIngredient: 4 cups shredded green cabbage (1/3 small cabbage)
10 dried Chinese black mushrooms, soaked in hot water for 20 minutes, drained, stems removed, caps thinly sliced
8 scallions, green part only, cut into 1-inch lengths
8 Mandarin pancakes or flour tortillas, steamed
recipeInstructions:
Marinade
2 1/2 tbsp reduced-sodium soy sauce
1 1/2 tbsp rice wine or sake
1 tsp sesame oil
Minced Seasonings
6 cloves garlic, minced
1 1/2 tbsp minced fresh ginger
Sauce
3 tablespoons chicken broth
2 tablespoons rice wine or sake
1 tablespoon soy sauce
1 tablespoon cornstarch
1/2 teaspoon sugar
1/4 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
Marinate armadillo:
1. In a medium-sized bowl, combine marinade ingredients.
2. Add armadillo and toss lightly to coat.
3. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Mix minced seasonings:
1. Combine garlic and ginger; set aside.
Make sauce:
1. Combine sauce ingredients in a small bowl and blend well; set aside.
Stir-fry mu shu filling:
1. Heat a wok or large skillet over high heat.
2. Add 1/2 tablespoon vegetable oil and heat until very hot.
3. Add the marinated armadillo and stir-fry until cooked through, about 2 minutes.
4. Remove with a slotted spoon and set aside.
5. Cook down any remaining juices to a glaze and add to the armadillo.
6. Add another 1/2 tablespoon oil to the wok and heat until very hot.
7. Add eggs and stir-fry, scrambling them until just dry. Remove and set aside.
8. Add the reserved minced seasoning and stir-fry until fragrant, 10 to 15 seconds.
9. Add cabbage and mushrooms and stir-fry until tender, about 2 minutes.
10. Pour in the reserved sauce mixture and stir constantly until thickened, about 1 minute.
11. Return the armadillo and eggs to the pan and toss until heated through.
12. Stir in scallions.
To serve:
1. Spread some hoisin sauce over a steamed pancake or tortilla.
2. Spoon some of the stir-fried mixture on top.
3. Roll up and eat.
